decisions.decision: Use plain render() from @testing-library/react, not renderWithProviders
alternatives_rejected: renderWithProviders — unnecessary since component has no context/provider dependencies

decisions.decision: Handle locale-dependent formatting by computing expected values with the same toLocaleString() calls in test assertions rather than hardcoding strings
alternatives_rejected: Mocking toLocaleString — adds complexity for no benefit
Hardcoding locale strings — brittle across environments
Using regex partial matches — less precise

decisions.decision: Test formatCurrency only through component integration (rendered output), not as separate unit tests in this file
alternatives_rejected: Duplicating formatCurrency unit tests — already covered in format.test.ts

decisions.decision: Explicitly import describe/it/expect from vitest, following established codebase convention
alternatives_rejected: Relying on vitest globals — works but breaks convention used in all 14 existing test files

decisions.decision: Test edge cases: zero values, large numbers (millions range for volume, thousands for trades), and typical mid-range values
alternatives_rejected: Testing negative numbers — not meaningful in prediction market context
Testing invalid dates — low priority and not in task scope

steps.task: Create test file at frontend/src/__tests__/AnalyticsSummaryCards.test.tsx with imports: vitest (describe, it, expect), @testing-library/react (render, screen), and the AnalyticsSummaryCards component
Write test: renders all four card labels (Total Activity, Total Volume, Unique Traders, Last Update) with typical mid-range summary data

Write test: renders correctly formatted values — totalTrades and uniqueTraders via .toLocaleString() comparison, totalVolume via formatCurrency output, lastUpdated via new Date().toLocaleString() comparison
Write test: renders the 'Last Update' caption text ('Accepted activity freshness for the selected market.') only under the Last Update card
Write test: handles zero values (totalTrades=0, totalVolume=0, uniqueTraders=0) — verifies component renders without errors and shows formatted zeros
Write test: handles large numbers — totalVolume in millions (triggers $X.XXM format), totalTrades in thousands (triggers locale thousands separator)
Run vitest on the new test file to verify all tests pass
